metadata.source: 524 audited sessions from iSchool Dashboard V2 (updated March 2026)
metadata.description: Per-subcategory false-positive rates from human auditor review. Used to filter phantom findings.

high_false_positive_subcategories.warning: 60% FP — Only flag if tutor NEVER used annotation tools during the entire session. Single instances are normal.
52% FP — require a pattern of 3+ consecutive minutes with zero student interaction.
54% FP — Tutor demonstrating code is NORMAL. Only flag if tutor FULLY took over and student did nothing.
66% FP — Arabic is the session language. Informal Arabic is NORMAL. Only flag actual profanity or insults.
55% FP — Often student-side. Only flag if tutor's OWN connection caused >3 session disruptions.
67% FP — Single impatient moment is not a flag. Require 3+ documented instances of harsh/frustrated tone.
58% FP — Wrong name once is not a flag. Only flag if tutor kept wrong name after correction.
80% FP — Do NOT flag virtual background / iSchool logo issues from frames. Zoom background detection from frames is unreliable.
77% FP — Do NOT flag 'no roadmap' unless tutor clearly had no lesson plan. Tutors give roadmap verbally.
48% FP — Partial completion is common. Only flag if less than 50% of curriculum was covered.
49% FP — Verify homework was actually skipped (not just done verbally) before flagging.
49% FP — Verify actual session duration from audio/video length, not transcript timestamps.
36% FP — Only flag if time management failure directly caused curriculum not being covered.
92% FP — NEVER flag 'not in fullscreen/slideshow mode'. This is a known phantom. DELETE any such finding.

reliable_subcategories.note: Reliable when tutor leaves mid-session for personal reasons.
Reliable when tutor starts without any greeting.
Reliable when tutor was clearly unprepared (multiple bugs, wrong content).

reliable_subcategories.subcategory: C-Tools used and Methodology (no annotations ever)
reliable_subcategories.note: Reliable only when tutor never used annotation tools across entire session.

false_positive_patterns.instruction: DELETE — background detection from Zoom frame screenshots is unreliable. 80% of such findings are wrong.
DELETE — Arabic informal speech is normal in tutoring. Only flag actual profanity or explicit insults.

false_positive_patterns.pattern: Zoom annotation tools — mouse instead of arrows
false_positive_patterns.instruction: Only keep if tutor NEVER used annotation tools in the entire session. Single or occasional mouse use is normal.
Convert to [Advice] only. Do NOT flag or deduct. Teaching methodology varies and is often appropriate.
Only flag if tutor's OWN connection caused 3+ disruptions. Distinguish from student-side issues.
Require 3+ documented timestamps of harsh tone. Single ambiguous moment = DELETE.
Only flag if tutor used wrong name repeatedly AFTER the student corrected them.
DELETE ALWAYS — top phantom pattern. Code editors shown in normal mode is correct.
DELETE unless tutor explicitly had wrong session content. Verbal roadmaps are acceptable.
Only keep if poor pacing directly caused curriculum not being covered. Otherwise use [Advice].
DELETE — student internet, student camera, student software = student's issue, not tutor's.
Convert to [Advice] only. Human reviewers do not deduct for this.

false_positive_patterns.pattern: Homework — reviewed verbally but not on dashboard
false_positive_patterns.instruction: Only flag if homework was completely skipped. Verbal review is acceptable.

flag_accuracy.note: Over half of all flags in V32 were marked inaccurate by human reviewers. Require very strong, timestamp-backed evidence before any flag.
